one_liner.value: Unified spend platform for corporate cards, expense management, bill pay, travel, and business banking with a REST API for programmatic access.

access.notes: API access is included in the free Essentials plan ($0/user/month). However, obtaining a Brex account has eligibility requirements (venture-backed startup or mid-market company, US incorporation, EIN, US operations). API tokens require account or card admin role within the organization. The Essentials plan clearly lists 'Brex API access' as a feature.

summary.value: REST API defined with OpenAPI specification. Multiple API categories: Accounting, Budgets, Expenses, Fields, Onboarding, Payments, Team, Transactions, Travel, Webhooks. Webhooks supported. Sandbox environment available. All APIs accept and return JSON, require HTTPS.

rationale.value: Brex has a well-documented public REST API with broad coverage of financial operations. Community MCP servers already exist (mcp-brex npm package, Composio toolkit with 84 tools). A developer who is a Brex customer with admin access can self-serve generate API tokens instantly from the dashboard at no additional cost (API access is included on the free Essentials plan). However, becoming a Brex customer requires meeting eligibility criteria (venture-backed, US entity, etc.) and having admin role within the organization. For qualified organizations and their developers, the API is fully buildable into an agent toolkit today.

s1.quote: Brex is the first fully unified spend platform — with global corporate cards, expense management, reimbursements, travel, and bill pay. ... Using the Brex API, you can power your internal tools and create custom workflows. Brex APIs use the REST architecture, are defined using the OpenAPI specification and use standard HTTP response codes and verbs. All APIs accept and return JSON and require HTTPS.
s1.title: Brex API Overview

s2.quote: Essentials: $0 user/month. Features include: Brex API access, Real-time reporting, Bill pay, Reimbursements. ... Premium: $12 user/month. ... Enterprise: Custom pricing.
s2.title: Brex Pricing Plans

s3.quote: To start making calls to Brex APIs, generate a user token from your Brex dashboard and pass it along in your API call headers. ... Sign in to dashboard.brex.com as an account admin or card admin. Go to Developer > Settings. ... If your company has not accepted the developer API agreement, please review the terms and conditions. These must be accepted to use the developer API.
s3.title: Developer authentication

s4.quote: Please contact our developer support team to be issued a client ID and client secret for partner authentication. ... Brex currently supports 2 different authorization grant types: ... Authorization Code Grant ... Client Credentials Grant
s4.title: Partner authentication

s5.quote: This guide assumes you are a Brex customer with admin access to your dashboard. ... To do so, add the header Authorization: Bearer <YOUR_TOKEN_FROM_STEP_1_HERE> and make a GET request to https://api.brex.com/v2/users/me
s5.title: Quickstart

s6.quote: Account and card admins can get started on the API by visiting Brex for Developers, our documentation hub, and reading through our documentation and example apps. Check your role permissions and if you're authorized, generate a user token from your Brex dashboard following these instructions.
s6.title: Brex API support page

s7.quote: All Brex applicants are required to have a US EIN issued by the IRS, a valid US incorporation, US operations, and a US physical address. ... Venture-backed startups: Include professionally funded, non-traditionally funded, and pre-funding companies. Mid-market Companies: Either a professionally-funded company with 20 or more employees or companies with 50 more employees that are not invested.
s7.title: Brex account requirements

s8.quote: Essentials is limited to 2 entities. ... Brex has the most restrictive eligibility requirements of any major corporate card and spend management platform.
s8.title: Brex Pricing: Plans, Costs, and What You Actually Pay

s9.quote: A MCP server for interacting with Brex expense tracking platform. ... A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for the Brex API. Optimized for safe, small, read-only responses with projection and batching.
s9.title: crazyrabbitLTC/mcp-brex-server

s10.quote: Securely connect your AI agents and chatbots (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, etc) with Brex MCP or direct API to automate expense reports, review transactions, manage company cards, and generate real-time spend analytics through natural language. ... Tools: 84
s10.title: Brex MCP Integration for AI Agents | Composio

s11.quote: Brex provides a financial platform integrated with APIs to manage company expenses, accounting, and business banking. ... It provides 6 tools that AI agents can use through the Model Context Protocol (MCP).
s11.title: Brex MCP Server & Skill

s12.quote: Brex MCP Server is a Model Context Protocol server that provides AI assistants read-only access to the Brex API for expenses, budgets, transactions, and accounts.
s12.title: Brex MCP Server | MCP Server

limitations: Could not verify if there is a free sandbox that doesn't require a Brex customer account. The developer portal mentions a staging environment but it is described as 'not a sandbox' and 'will not work with customer tokens'.
Could not confirm specific rate limits from official docs.
Did not find an official Brex MCP server - only community-created ones.
